name: Oktoberfest
description: Bavaria's beer festival — its royal origin, traditions, tents, and hearty stats.

questions.q: In what year was the first Oktoberfest held?

source.notes: Originally celebrating the wedding of Crown Prince Ludwig (later King Ludwig I) and Princess Therese.

questions.q: Which royal wedding originally inspired Oktoberfest?
choices: Crown Prince Ludwig and Princess Therese
King Ludwig II and Marie of Prussia

source.original_a: The royal wedding of Crown Prince Ludwig and Princess Therese

questions.q: Which German city hosts the world's largest Oktoberfest each year?
What is the local Bavarian nickname for the Oktoberfest fairgrounds?
About how long does the modern Oktoberfest run?

source.notes: 16-18 days; ends the first Sunday of October.

questions.q: What is the traditional shouted phrase when the Mayor of Munich taps the first Oktoberfest keg?
How many breweries are legally allowed to serve beer at Oktoberfest?

source.original_a: Only six Munich breweries are allowed to serve Oktoberfest beer
source.notes: Augustiner, Hacker-Pschorr, Hofbräu, Löwenbräu, Paulaner, Spaten.

questions.q: Which beer style is traditionally served at Oktoberfest?

source.notes: Modern Festbier is lighter; traditional Märzen is fuller-bodied.

questions.q: What is the standard Oktoberfest beer mug size?
About how many liters of beer are served annually at Oktoberfest?
About how many people attend Oktoberfest in Munich each year?
Which traditional men's leather Bavarian shorts are worn at Oktoberfest?

source.notes: Often paired with knee-high socks and Haferl shoes.

questions.q: What is the traditional Bavarian women's Oktoberfest dress called?

source.notes: Apron bow position signals relationship status — left=single, right=taken, middle=virgin/young, back=widow.

questions.q: What bow placement on the dirndl traditionally signals 'taken'?

source.notes: Left = single, Right = taken, Center = virgin, Back = widow/server.

questions.q: Which event canceled Oktoberfest in both 1854 and 1873?
Why is Oktoberfest mostly held in September?

source.notes: Pulled forward in the 1870s for warmer weather; ends the first Sunday of October.

questions.q: What is a 'Brezel' as commonly served at Oktoberfest?

source.notes: Usually huge, salt-crusted, served with sweet mustard.

questions.q: Which song is the most-played 'beer drinking' anthem in Oktoberfest tents?

source.notes: Full title: 'Ein Prosit der Gemütlichkeit' — a toast to coziness/conviviality.

questions.q: Approximately how many large beer tents (Festzelte) typically operate at Oktoberfest?

source.notes: About 14 large tents plus ~20 smaller tents.

questions.q: The Theresienwiese — Munich's Oktoberfest fairground — is named for which person?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: replaced dubious saint-feast-day premise. Theresienwiese is named for Princess Therese, whose 1810 wedding to Crown Prince Ludwig I started Oktoberfest.
